easy.title: Two Sum
easy.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
hints: Think about what complement you need for each number
A hash map can give you O(1) lookup
One pass through the array is sufficient

easy.title: Valid Parentheses
easy.description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if: Open brackets must be closed by the same type of brackets, and open brackets must be closed in the correct order.
hints: Use a stack data structure
Push opening brackets, pop for closing
Check if the popped element matches

easy.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
easy.description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hints: Use a dummy head node
Compare elements one by one
Don't forget the remaining elements

easy.title: Maximum Subarray
easy.description: Given an integer array nums, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.
hints: Think about Kadane's algorithm
At each position, decide: extend current subarray or start new
Track both current sum and global maximum

easy.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
easy.description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock.
hints: Track the minimum price seen so far
At each day, calculate potential profit
Keep a running maximum profit

easy.title: Valid Palindrome
easy.description: A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward. Given a string s, return true if it is a palindrome, or false otherwise.
hints: Use two pointers from both ends
Skip non-alphanumeric characters
Compare characters case-insensitively

medium.title: LRU Cache
medium.description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. Implement the LRUCache class with get and put operations in O(1) time complexity.
hints: Combine a hash map with a doubly linked list
Hash map gives O(1) access, linked list gives O(1) removal/insertion
Move accessed nodes to the front

medium.title: Number of Islands
medium.description: Given an m x n 2D binary grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
hints: Use BFS or DFS from each unvisited '1'
Mark visited cells to avoid counting twice
Each BFS/DFS call discovers one complete island

medium.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
medium.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
hints: Use a queue (BFS)
Process all nodes at current level before moving to next
Track the size of each level

medium.title: Coin Change
medium.description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount.
hints: Think dynamic programming - build up from smaller amounts
dp[i] = minimum coins needed for amount i
For each amount, try all coin denominations

medium.title: 3Sum
medium.description: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.
hints: Sort the array first
Fix one element, use two pointers for the other two
Skip duplicates to avoid repeated triplets

medium.title: Word Search
medium.description: Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring.
hints: Use DFS/backtracking from each cell
Mark cells as visited during exploration
Restore cells when backtracking

medium.title: Top K Frequent Elements
medium.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
hints: Count frequencies with a hash map
Use a min-heap of size k
Or use bucket sort for O(n) solution

medium.title: Course Schedule
medium.description: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take. Some courses may have prerequisites. Given the total number of courses and a list of prerequisite pairs, determine if it is possible to finish all courses.
hints: This is cycle detection in a directed graph
Use topological sort (BFS with in-degrees or DFS)
If a cycle exists, not all courses can be completed

hard.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
hard.description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).
hints: Binary search on the smaller array
Find a partition where left elements ≤ right elements
The partition divides combined array into two equal halves

hard.title: Merge K Sorted Lists
hard.description: You are given an array of k linked-lists lists, each linked-list is sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.
hints: Use a min-heap to track the smallest element across all lists
Always pop the minimum element and push the next from that list
Alternatively, divide and conquer by merging pairs

hard.title: Trapping Rain Water
hard.description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
hints: Water at each position = min(max_left, max_right) - height[i]
Use two pointers from both ends
Track max heights from each direction

hard.title: Word Ladder
hard.description: Given two words, beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary's word list, return the number of words in the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord, or 0 if no such sequence exists. Each transformed word must exist in the word list.
hints: BFS finds shortest path in unweighted graph
Each word is a node, edges connect words differing by one letter
Pre-process words into pattern groups for efficiency

hard.title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
hard.description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree. Serialization is the process of converting a data structure or object into a sequence of bits so that it can be stored or transmitted.
hints: Use preorder traversal with null markers
Use a delimiter to separate values
For deserialization, use a queue/iterator approach

hard.title: Alien Dictionary
hard.description: There is a new alien language that uses the English alphabet. The order among letters is unknown. You are given a list of strings words from the alien dictionary, where the strings are sorted lexicographically. Derive the order of letters in this language.
hints: Compare adjacent words to find ordering constraints
Build a directed graph of character dependencies
Topological sort gives the character order

easy.title: Design URL Shortener
easy.description: Design a URL shortening service like bit.ly. The service should generate a short URL for a given long URL, redirect short URLs to original URLs, handle high read traffic, and support custom short URLs.

easy.title: Design Rate Limiter
easy.description: Design a rate limiter that can be used as middleware in a distributed system. It should support multiple rate limiting algorithms and handle distributed environments.
key_points: Token bucket vs sliding window algorithms

easy.title: Design Key-Value Store
easy.description: Design a distributed key-value store that supports put(key, value) and get(key) operations with high availability and partition tolerance.

medium.title: Design Twitter/News Feed
medium.description: Design a social media news feed system. Users can post tweets, follow other users, and see a combined feed of tweets from people they follow, sorted by time.
key_points: Fan-out on write vs fan-out on read

medium.title: Design Notification System
medium.description: Design a scalable notification system that supports multiple channels (push, SMS, email) with delivery guarantees, user preferences, and rate limiting.

medium.title: Design Chat System
medium.description: Design a real-time chat system supporting 1-on-1 and group chats with message history, online presence, read receipts, and media sharing.

medium.title: Design E-Commerce Checkout
medium.description: Design a checkout system for an e-commerce platform handling inventory management, payment processing, order management, and high traffic during sales events.

medium.title: Design Search Autocomplete
medium.description: Design a search autocomplete/typeahead system that provides real-time suggestions as users type, handling billions of queries per day.

hard.title: Design YouTube/Video Platform
hard.description: Design a video sharing platform supporting video upload, transcoding, streaming, recommendations, and handling millions of concurrent viewers.

hard.title: Design Google Maps
hard.description: Design a navigation system with real-time traffic, route calculation, ETA estimation, and handling millions of concurrent users.

hard.title: Design Distributed Message Queue
hard.description: Design a distributed message queue like Kafka supporting ordered delivery, at-least-once semantics, consumer groups, and handling millions of messages per second.

easy.title: Tell me about yourself
easy.description: Provide a 2-minute summary of your background, key achievements, and what motivates you. Focus on relevance to the role.

easy.title: Why this company?
easy.description: Explain why you want to work at this specific company and role. Show research and genuine interest.

easy.title: Describe your biggest achievement
easy.description: Share your most impactful professional achievement. Use STAR format with measurable results.

easy.title: How do you handle disagreements?
easy.description: Describe a specific situation where you disagreed with a colleague or manager and how you resolved it.

medium.title: Tell me about a time you failed
medium.description: Describe a significant failure, what you learned, and how you applied those lessons. Show self-awareness and growth.

medium.title: Describe leading a project with ambiguity
medium.description: Tell me about a time you had to lead a project with unclear requirements or limited information.

medium.title: Time you had to make a decision with incomplete data
medium.description: Describe a situation where you had to make an important decision without all the information you wanted.

medium.title: How did you handle a tight deadline?
medium.description: Tell me about a time you had to deliver a critical project under a very tight timeline.

medium.title: Describe influencing without authority
medium.description: Tell me about a time you had to influence a decision when you didn't have direct authority.

medium.title: Tell me about improving a process
medium.description: Describe a time you identified an inefficient process and improved it. What was the measurable impact?

medium.title: Dealing with a difficult team member
medium.description: Tell me about a time you worked with someone who was difficult to work with. How did you handle it?

medium.title: Prioritizing competing demands
medium.description: Describe a time you had multiple high-priority tasks and had to decide what to focus on first.

hard.title: Largest scope project you've led
hard.description: Tell me about the largest scope project you've led end-to-end. How did you manage complexity, stakeholders, and delivery?

hard.title: Time you challenged a senior leader
hard.description: Describe a time you disagreed with a VP or senior leader. How did you approach it and what happened?

hard.title: Building something from zero to one
hard.description: Tell me about a time you built something completely new - from ideation through launch and beyond.

hard.title: Handling a production crisis
hard.description: Describe the most critical production incident you've handled. Walk through your thought process and actions.

hard.title: Making a trade-off between teams
hard.description: Tell me about a time you had to make a decision that benefited one team at the expense of another.

hard.title: Driving organizational change
hard.description: Describe a time you drove significant change across an organization. How did you get buy-in and handle resistance?

hard.title: Long-term technical vision
hard.description: Tell me about a time you defined and drove a long-term technical strategy. How did you balance short-term needs with long-term goals?

hard.title: Mentoring and developing engineers
hard.description: Tell me about your approach to developing engineers. Give a specific example of someone you mentored and their growth trajectory.
